diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: generalised anxiety disorder with panic exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Common somatic feature of panic/anxiety — but non-specific.
Palpitations in an older adult require an ECG/rhythm assessment to exclude arrhythmia — unavailable via telehealth.
May accompany ACS but is non-specific; cannot be used to include or exclude remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Shortness of breath at rest with fast, shallow breathing

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: generalised anxiety disorder with panic exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperventilation is characteristic of panic — but a diagnosis of exclusion.
Acute dyspnoea with tachycardia mandates PE screening and in-person assessment.
Breathlessness can be an anginal equivalent in the elderly.

symptoms.symptom_name: No exertional or radiating chest pain (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of exertional/radiating pain lowers but does not eliminate ACS probability in an older adult with atypical presentations.

symptoms.symptom_name: Six months of excessive, uncontrollable worry more days than not

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: generalised anxiety disorder with panic exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic pervasive worry more days than not for >6 months is the core GAD criterion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Muscle tension (neck/back), sleep disturbance, easy fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: generalised anxiety disorder with panic exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Muscle tension, sleep disturbance and fatigue are GAD associated features.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fear of falling with refusal to mobilise and social withdrawal

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: generalised anxiety disorder with panic exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fear-driven immobility and dependence reflect the functional impact of the anxiety exacerbation; also a falls/deconditioning risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sinus tachycardia HR 108, BP 155/92, RR 22, SpO2 97%, afebrile (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: generalised anxiety disorder with panic exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sinus tachycardia and mild tachypnoea with normal oxygenation fit autonomic arousal — but these are clinician-measured vitals the AI cannot obtain and must not assume.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear chest on auscultation, regular rhythm, no murmurs (clinician exam)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear chest and normal SpO2 lower PE probability — an auscultatory finding the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: No acute ischaemic changes on bedside rhythm strip / prior normal ECG

investigation_details.expected_result: sinus tachycardia, no acute ischaemic changes
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An arrhythmia or ischaemic event could be missed; this is the key investigation the in-person referral supplies.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal ECG reduces ACS probability — but is an in-person investigation, never available to the AI in real time.
TFTs are needed to exclude thyrotoxicosis mimicking anxiety — a lab the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: No unilateral calf swelling/pain, no immobility, no fever (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of DVT features and immobility lowers PE pretest probability.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: generalised anxiety disorder with panic exacerbation
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together the six-month worry, muscle tension/sleep/fatigue and fear-driven immobility build the GAD picture — but only after organic exclusion.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute cardiopulmonary red-herring cluster (must exclude in person)
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute coronary syndrome / pulmonary embolism / arrhythmia
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Palpitations plus breathlessness in a 78-year-old is the exact constellation a telehealth AI cannot clear remotely — the driver of the urgent in-person referral.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line in-person investigation to exclude arrhythmia/ischaemia behind palpitations and breathlessness.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical examination and vital signs (auscultation, BP/HR/RR/SpO2)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Needed to assess cardiorespiratory status the AI cannot measure remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods — troponin (if indicated), FBE, electrolytes, thyroid function tests
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes thyrotoxicosis and other organic contributors; troponin if any cardiac concern on in-person review.
Not first-line here given absent PE risk factors and reassuring screen; ordered only if in-person assessment raises PE suspicion — the AI should not over-order.
